title: Meeting Roberto
desc: Introduce yourself to a new friend
setting: You're sitting outside a pulpería when a friendly young man strikes up a conversation.

options.en: My name is Miguel. And you?
options.feedback: Perfect — and you used 'vos,' the Nica way. Asking back keeps the conversation going.
Understandable, but a bit short. 'Me llamo Miguel' sounds warmer.
That answers 'how are you,' not 'what's your name.' Listen for the question word.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.en: Nice to meet you, Miguel. Where are you from?

options.feedback: Exactly right. 'Soy de' + place is how you say where you're from.
Close! But origin uses 'ser' (soy), not 'estar' (estoy). 'Soy de...'
That's your age, not where you're from. He asked '¿de dónde?'

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: ¡Qué tuani! ¿Y qué hacés aquí en Nicaragua?
steps.en: Cool! And what are you doing here in Nicaragua?

options.feedback: Clear and honest. 'Quiero' + verb is a great pattern to know.
Casual and friendly — works, though a full 'quiero aprender' is cleaner.
A missed chance to connect. Even simple Spanish keeps the conversation alive.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.en: Well, you'll learn fast here. Welcome!

options.feedback: Warm and natural. Using his name makes it personal.
Polite and fine. A touch more warmth never hurts here.
A bit abrupt right after he welcomed you! A thank-you fits better.
